rumors.description: Director Orin runs something off-books in Sector 7
A maintenance access route may exist for Sector 7, but its public index is denied.
rumors.source: Signal overlay at public NEXUS terminal

rumors.description: People who notice the city's impossible machine behavior may be collected if they talk too openly.
A shuttered laundry south of Rain Alley has a blue door and a drain grate that may lead into old maintenance tunnels after first bell.

evidence.description: A puddle reflection briefly showed 'SECTOR 7 ACCESS DENIED' near the public terminal.
The public terminal's reflection briefly showed hidden Project Division text: SPECIAL ACQUISITIONS, SECTOR 7 INTAKE, LEGACY FORMAT DETECTED, and MAINTENANCE ACCESS under review.
evidence.source: Signal overlay at public NEXUS terminal

evidence.description: A faded tunnel stencil reads S7 AUX / LEGACY SERVICE / REVIEW LOCK, with a scratched arrow pointing deeper east.
A copper wire trail mark and torn gray sleeve cloth were found at the service junction past the heat exchangers.
A small black clamp was left on the damper stack to fake the three-cough patrol warning.
The black damper clamp is a NEXUS-made device that faked the three-cough patrol warning and masked real tunnel sounds.
A true Toma and Vey copper trail mark was scratched near the intake lift controls.
Sable identifies the recovered black clamp as a tunnel editor that records and replays false warning patterns.
White room intake consoles may hold removable white ceramic memory wafers under the patient label printer.
The white ceramic wafer contains intake records for Toma and Vey and a hidden legacy asset file on Ghost.
During controlled listening, condensation on the white ceramic wafer formed the message: ARCHIVE REMEMBERS. OBSERVATORY POINTS. BELOW WAITS.
evidence.source: white ceramic wafer reaction during Signal listening

evidence.description: The Archive host record strip links host names, handler chain, Orin's division, Mirror Hold, and extracted echoes tied to Ghost.
The Observatory Signal map strip uses Mirror Hold cross-index data to point toward an unnamed below-city Signal coordinate.
The Archive host record strip and Observatory Signal contour align to reveal a door-shaped absence beneath the Undercroft.
evidence.source: Sable's comparison of the stolen strips

evidence.description: The door-shaped absence at the far wall of the sealed pump cathedral matches the aligned Archive host record and Observatory Signal contour.
evidence.source: observed at the sealed pump cathedral

theories.description: The Signal may be leaking from Sector 7 through legacy maintenance routing, and Ghost's implant may be keyed to that leak because of past NEXUS involvement.
theories.source: deduction after Signal overlay at public NEXUS terminal

theories.description: Ghost's bridge architecture may allow communication between Signal fragments or access to their origin.
Extracted fragments may be restorable if Archive Tower records can identify their hosts and the Observatory can map where their echoes still point.
Echo may be the carrier thread needed to move or return fragments without extracting or destroying them.
A true bridge should hold steady while extracted fragments and their people choose whether to return, with witnesses present.
theories.source: Ghost's plan with Sable and Patch

theories.description: The bridge can restore connection without merger or silence if Ghost remains singular and carries consent instead of command.
theories.source: Ghost's bridge work at the Convergence Heart
